steps.title: Start with the given equation
steps.content: The given equation is $\frac{y - 28}{x} = p$. To express $y$ in terms of $p$ and $x$, isolate $y$.

steps.title: Isolate y algebraically
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $x$:
$$y - 28 = px$$

Add 28 to both sides:
$$y = px + 28$$

This is Choice B — $y = px + 28$.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• A ($y = 28x + p$): Rearranging gives $\frac{y-p}{x} = 28$, not $p$. Eliminated.
  • C ($y = 28px$): Rearranging gives $\frac{y}{28x} = p$, not $\frac{y-28}{x}$. Eliminated.
  • D ($y = \frac{px}{28}$): Rearranging gives $\frac{28y}{x} = p$, not $\frac{y-28}{x}$. Eliminated.
That matches Choice B — $y = px + 28$.
